name: SAUL CENTERS, INC.
business: Saul Centers, Inc. is a real estate investment trust (REIT) that owns and operates shopping center and mixed-use properties, primarily grocery-anchored shopping centers and transit-oriented residential mixed-use developments in the Washington, DC/Baltimore metropolitan area. The company generates revenue from leasing retail, office, and residential space to tenants.

exposures.sentence: The Company's primary strategy is to continue to focus on diversification of its assets through development of transit-oriented, residential mixed-use projects and expansion of and additions to its grocery-anchored shopping centers in the Washington, DC/Baltimore metropolitan area.

Finally, retailers at our Shopping Centers face increasing competition from outlet stores, online retailers, discount shopping clubs and other forms of marketing goods, such as direct mail, internet marketing and telemarketing.

risks relating to cybersecurity and potential future uses of artificial intelligence ( AI ), including disruption to our business and operations, reputational risk, regulatory risk, and exposure to liabilities from tenants, employees, capital providers, and other third parties;

risks relating to cybersecurity and potential future uses of artificial intelligence ( AI ), including disruption to our business and operations, reputational risk, regulatory risk, and exposure to liabilities from tenants, employees, capital providers, and other third parties;
The Company is developing Twinbrook Quarter Phase I located in Rockville, Maryland.

catalysts.event: Twinbrook Quarter Phase I residential opening
catalysts.sentence: The Milton at Twinbrook Quarter opened and residential tenants began moving in on October 1, 2024.
Hampden House opened and residential tenants began moving in on October 1, 2025.

catalysts.event: Wegmans supermarket opening at Twinbrook Quarter
catalysts.sentence: The Wegmans supermarket at Twinbrook Quarter opened for business on June 25, 2025.

catalysts.event: Twinbrook Quarter Phase I residential leasing milestone
catalysts.sentence: As of February 23, 2026, 440 of the 452 (97.3%) residential units were leased and occupied.

catalysts.event: Remaining investment to complete Twinbrook Quarter Phase I
catalysts.sentence: Excluding imputed capitalized interest, the remaining investment to complete Twinbrook Quarter Phase I is not expected to exceed $9.9 million.

catalysts.event: Remaining investment to complete Hampden House
catalysts.sentence: Excluding imputed capitalized interest, the remaining investment to complete the project is not expected to exceed $6.8 million.
During 2025, the Company entered into a lease with Publix for a new grocery store, which we will construct, at Ashland Square in Prince William County, Virginia.

tamClaims.claim: Pipeline of entitled sites for development of up to 2,500 apartment units and 850,000 square feet of retail and office space
tamClaims.sentence: The Company has a pipeline of entitled sites in its portfolio, some of which are currently Shopping Centers, for development of up to 2,500 apartment units and 850,000 square feet of retail and office space.

tamClaims.claim: Twinbrook Quarter site development potential of 1,865 residential units, 473,000 square feet of retail space, and 431,000 square feet of office space
tamClaims.sentence: The development potential of all phases of the entire 18.4 acre Twinbrook Quarter site totals 1,865 residential units, 473,000 square feet of retail space, and 431,000 square feet of office space.

tamClaims.claim: Ashland Square expected to comprise approximately 124,000 square feet of retail space including 50,325 square foot Publix and additional pad sites
tamClaims.sentence: When complete, Ashland Square is expected to ultimately comprise approximately 124,000 square feet of retail space including the 50,325 square foot Publix, three existing pad sites, four additional pad sites and approximately 30,000 square feet of small shop space.
